ideation: This sequence simulates the action of shaving with a razor. The dominant hand (right, for right-handed users) will mimic the holding and drawing motion of the razor across the face. The left arm will remain in a supporting, relaxed position. Frame 1 will begin with the right arm slightly raised, wrist angled to hold the razor, as if ready to start. The left arm will remain at the side. Frame 2 will show the right arm moving across the face. The wrist rotates slightly to complete the shaving motion. The left arm will remain at the side. Frame 3 will be a repetition of frame 1, returning to the starting position. Frame 4 will be a continuation of the shaving motion, but in a different area of the face, again showing the right arm and wrist moving to simulate shaving. Frame 5, the final frame, will show both arms returning to a neutral position.

frames.beat: right arm raises, ready to shave
right arm sweeps down, shaving the cheek
right arm returns, preparing for the next stroke
right arm shaves again, a different angle
both arms rest, the shave is complete
